vasena.meaning: on account of, because of; by means of
vasena.gram: masculine noun, instrumental singular (of vasa, used adverbially)

vasena vatteyya.meaning: you should bring under control; should proceed by mastery over
breakdown: By means of, under the control of (instrumental).
Should proceed, should conduct (optative of vattati).

vassasahassameva.gram: neuter noun, accusative singular + emphatic particle (compound)

vassasatassa.meaning: of a hundred years; of a century

vassaṁvuṭṭhānaṁ.meaning: the end of the rains retreat; emergence from the rains residence
breakdown: The rain; the rains retreat (vassa residence).

vasī.meaning: having mastery; one having power over (the senses)
vasī.gram: adjective (vasin); the form also serves as aorist of vasati “dwelt”

vasīgaṇena.meaning: with a group of self-controlled ones; together with the company of masters (of the senses)

vattabbayuttakālaṁ.meaning: the proper time for what should be said; a fitting occasion to speak
breakdown: What should be spoken (future passive participle of vadati).

vattabbe.meaning: to be said; what should be spoken
vattabbe.gram: future passive participle of vadati/vatti, locative singular

vattayamappamatto.meaning: practising this diligently; keeping it up without negligence
breakdown: Practising, keeping up (present participle of vatteti, with connecting m).

vatteyyāsi.meaning: you should proceed, occupy yourself with, practise
vatteyyāsi.gram: verb, optative 2nd person singular of vattati

vatthusamatte.meaning: in the likeness of the object; with regard to the equality of the matter

vattāro.gram: masculine agent noun, nominative plural (of vattar, from vacati)

vatāyasmantaṁyeva.meaning: indeed just the Venerable one; truly the Venerable himself
breakdown: Emphatic particle: “just” (a connecting y bridges the words).
Root: to go; vaya “passing away, decay”.

vaḷavābhirathena.meaning: by an excellent chariot drawn by mares

vaṅgīsasaṁyuttaṁ, sagāthāvaggo,.meaning: the Vaṅgīsa Saṁyutta, in the Sagāthāvagga of the Saṁyuttanikāya (a canonical reference)
breakdown: The connected discourses with the elder Vaṅgīsa.
The Section with Verses (of the Saṁyuttanikāya).

vaṇṇapokkharatāya.meaning: with beauty of complexion; with a lotus-like appearance

vaṇṇemi.gram: verb, 1st person singular present of vaṇṇeti

vedagu.meaning: one established in the experience of sensation; master of knowledge
breakdown: Knowledge; in this tradition: the felt experience, sensation (from √vid “to know, to feel”).
Gone to, arrived at (from √gam): one who has reached.
vedagu.note: divergence: general dictionaries gloss vedagū as “master of (Vedic) knowledge”; the lexicon reads it experientially as one established in the experience of sensation.

vedallakathaṁ.meaning: discussion by way of analysis and classification (vedalla-talk)
breakdown: Classification, analytical exposition (one of the nine classes of the teaching).

vedanā.meaning: bodily sensation; sensations as they manifest on the body
breakdown: Root: to know, to feel, to experience.
Action-noun suffix: vedanā, “what is felt” — sensation.
vedanā.goenka: Vedanā is bodily sensation — not mere “feeling” or emotion. On an experiential level it can be observed how an unwholesome intention, which may manifest in unwholesome acts of body and speech, first reveals itself within as an analogous disagreeable bodily sensation that would usually be ignored; at this point one can halt the intended act, realising it as harmful. Similarly, a wholesome intention may manifest in agreeable bodily sensations. The tradition stresses the central importance of sensations: when one does not react to them with hankering, longing or repugnance, but maintains calmness and equanimity of mind, the habitual reactive pattern of responding with craving is reduced — and with non-reactive observation of the arising and passing of neutral bodily sensations even the dormant tendency of ignorance is reduced. Vedanā is the object of the second foundation of mindfulness, vedanānupassanā.
vedanā.note: divergence: never to be rendered “feeling”, “emotion”, “sentiment” or “perception” — the tradition insists on “bodily sensation”.

veditabbo.meaning: to be realized; to be known, experienced
veditabbo.gram: future passive participle of vedeti (√vid), masculine nominative singular

velāanatikkamo.meaning: not overstepping the limit; not passing beyond the boundary

velāyaṁ.meaning: at this time; on this occasion
velāyaṁ.gram: feminine noun, locative singular (velā + ayaṁ in the lexicon's parse)
breakdown: This (the lexicon parses the form as velā + ayaṁ; it also reads as the locative singular velāyaṁ).

venakule.meaning: in a family of basket-makers; in a bamboo-worker clan

vepullamagamāsi.meaning: went to increase; attained full development
vepullamagamāsi.gram: verb phrase, aorist 3rd person singular (vepullaṁ + agamāsi)
breakdown: Increase, full development (accusative; with connecting m).

vepullāya.gram: neuter abstract noun, dative singular (of vepulla)

vesārajjappatto.meaning: having attained confidence; having reached self-assurance
breakdown: Having reached, attained (past participle of pāpuṇāti; with sandhi doubling of p).
Root: to do, make — vyākaraṇa, “explanation, exposition”.

veyyākaraṇasmiṁ.meaning: in the exposition; in the explanation

veḷuvane kalandakanivāpe.meaning: in the Bamboo Grove, at the squirrels' feeding ground (near Rājagaha)
veḷuvane kalandakanivāpe.summary: A monastery site near Rājagaha, a favourite dwelling place of the Buddha.
breakdown: The squirrels' feeding ground (kalandaka “squirrel” + nivāpa “feeding place”).

vibbhantacitto.meaning: with a wandering mind; confused, wavering in mind
breakdown: Roaming, gone astray, confused (past participle of vibbhamati).

vibhajantassā.gram: present participle of vibhajati, genitive singular
breakdown: Root: to divide; vibhajati “to analyse, classify”.

vibhajissāmi.meaning: I will distinguish; I will analyse in detail
vibhajissāmi.gram: verb, future 1st person singular of vibhajati

vibhajjabyākaraṇīyo.meaning: to be answered after analysis; (a question) requiring an analytical explanation
breakdown: To be explained, requiring explanation (future passive participle of byākaroti).

vibhajjavādī.meaning: one who teaches by analysis; an analyst — a designation of the followers of the Buddha, the Theravādins
vibhajjavādī.goenka: Vibhajjavādī — “one whose doctrine proceeds by analysis” — is a term for those who follow the teaching of the Buddha, the Theravādins: the doctrine of reasoned, analytical examination rather than dogmatic assertion.

vibhavataṇhā.meaning: craving for non-becoming; craving for annihilation
vibhavataṇhā.summary: One of the three kinds of taṇhā in the second Noble Truth, alongside kāmataṇhā and bhavataṇhā.
vibhavataṇhā.goenka: Vibhavataṇhā is the craving for non-becoming — the thirst for annihilation, for existence to cease. Together with kāmataṇhā (craving for sensual pleasures) and bhavataṇhā (craving for becoming), it forms the three cravings identified in the second Noble Truth as the origin of suffering.
